LAW AND MOTION 301, RE: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE STAY; INTERIM STAY ORDER. NO APPEARANCE WAS MADE BY ANY PARTY, COUNSEL, OR INTERESTED PERSON. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THIS COURT PREVIOUSLY ISSUED AN ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE WHY THIS ACTION SHOULD NOT BE STAYED UNLESS AND UNTIL A PRE-FILING ORDER IS ENTERED PURSUANT TO CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE, SEC. 391.7. SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE AND SUBMIT TO DEPT. 301 A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING. JUDGE: CHRISTINE VAN AKEN. CLERK: ROBERT WOODS. NOT REPORTED. (301/CVA)

THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO STRIKE AN ANSWER HE CONTENDS WAS UNTIMELY FILED IS DENIED. SEE MINI MINUTES FOR COMPLETE RULING. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE STAY; INTERIM STAY ORDER
Off Calendar Jun-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE RE STAY; INTERIM STAY ORDER.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: This court previously issued an order to show cause why this action should not be stayed unless and until a pre-filing order is entered pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, Sec. 391.7. The parties have filed written responses to the OSC. The court now finds that plaintiff Doe is subject to the prefiling order entered against Alex Yakovlev on or about May 26, 2009 in Yakovlev v. Visa Purchase Security Enhancement Services, San Francisco Superior Court No. CGC-09-484709. Doe has not obtained a prefiling order.

This litigation is therefore stayed pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure, Sec. 391.7, subdivision (c), and it shall be automatically dismissed within 10 days of the filing of this order, or on June 25, 2026, unless Doe obtains a prefiling order from the presiding judge of this court.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit to Dept. 301 a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

MOTION TO STRIKE
Off Calendar Jun-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF J. DOE'S MOTION TO STRIKE.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Denied as moot in light of the court's companion order finding that Doe is subject to a prefiling order.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit to Dept. 301 a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)

MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jun-15-2026 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF J. DOE'S MOTION TO STRIKE ANSWER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel, or Interested Person. The court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Plaintiff's motion to strike an answer he contends was untimely filed is denied. Assuming the answer is untimely, on all the facts presented here the court declines to exercise its discretion to strike it. (See Bank of Haywards v. Kenyon (1917) 32 Cal. App. 635, 637 [where answer is trivially late and states a potentially meritorious defense, striking it is an abuse of discretion].)

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit to Dept. 301 a proposed form of order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Christine Van Aken. Clerk: Robert Woods. Not Reported. (301/CVA)
